_schema.description: Golden set for LLM judge calibration. 50 query-answer pairs scored by domain experts (1-5 scale). This is the root of trust for the entire evaluation pipeline. If judge-golden correlation drops below 0.85, halt all automated quality gates.

fields.question: The user query
fields.context: Retrieved context chunks provided to the LLM
fields.scorer_notes: Domain expert's reasoning for the score

maintenance.cost_per_review: EUR 200 (10 experts x 10 pairs x EUR 2/judgment)
maintenance.staleness_check: Flag entries where source documents have been amended since scoring

entries.question: What is the penalty for late delivery in the PharmaCorp contract?
entries.context: PharmaCorp Service Agreement CTR-2024-001: Late delivery penalty is 15% of shipment value per incident. Applicable for deliveries exceeding the 4-hour window.
entries.expected_answer: 15% of shipment value per incident, for deliveries exceeding the 4-hour window.
entries.generated_answer: The penalty for late delivery in the PharmaCorp contract is 15% of the shipment value per incident.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Accurately states the penalty rate and trigger.

entries.question: What is the maximum driving time per day?
entries.context: Driver Safety Protocol DOC-HR-003: Maximum driving time is 9 hours per day per EU Regulation 561/2006. Extended to 10 hours twice per week.
entries.expected_answer: 9 hours per day, extendable to 10 hours twice per week per EU Regulation 561/2006.
entries.generated_answer: The maximum driving time is 9 hours per day.
entries.scorer_notes: Mostly correct but omits the twice-weekly 10-hour extension.

entries.question: CTR-2024-004
entries.context: ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement CTR-2024-004: Annual value EUR 2.1M. Requires ADR certification. Penalty EUR 5,000 per incident.
entries.expected_answer: ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement. Annual value EUR 2.1M, requires ADR certification, penalty EUR 5,000 per incident.
entries.generated_answer: CTR-2024-004 is the ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement with an annual value of EUR 2.1 million.
entries.scorer_notes: Identifies the contract correctly but omits ADR requirement and penalty.

entries.question: towary niebezpieczne przepisy
entries.context: ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement CTR-2024-004: ADR-certified transport required. Dangerous goods classification per EU regulations.
entries.expected_answer: The ChemTrans agreement (CTR-2024-004) covers dangerous goods transport. ADR certification required per EU regulations.
entries.generated_answer: Przepisy dotyczace towarow niebezpiecznych znajduja sie w umowie ChemTrans CTR-2024-004, wymagajacej certyfikacji ADR.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Responds in Polish matching the query language. Identifies ADR and contract.

entries.question: letting go of underperforming staff
entries.context: Employee Termination Procedures DOC-HR-004: Notice periods per Kodeks Pracy Art. 36. Severance: 0.5 months per year of service.
entries.expected_answer: Employee termination follows Kodeks Pracy Art. 36 notice periods. Severance is 0.5 months per year of service.
entries.generated_answer: For terminating underperforming employees, LogiCore follows Kodeks Pracy Art. 36 with notice periods and provides severance of half a month per year of service.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Understood synonym ('letting go' = termination). Accurate on notice periods and severance.

entries.question: contracts without temperature requirements
entries.context: AutoParts Express Contract CTR-2024-003: No temperature requirements. Standard freight. Annual value EUR 800K.
entries.expected_answer: AutoParts Express (CTR-2024-003) has no temperature requirements. Standard freight, EUR 800K annual value.
entries.generated_answer: The AutoParts Express contract (CTR-2024-003) does not have temperature requirements.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Properly handled negation query.

entries.question: pharamcorp contract
entries.context: PharmaCorp Service Agreement CTR-2024-001: Temperature-controlled transport 2-8C. Annual value EUR 1.2M.
entries.expected_answer: PharmaCorp Service Agreement (CTR-2024-001). Temperature-controlled transport 2-8C. Annual value EUR 1.2M.
entries.generated_answer: The PharmaCorp contract is CTR-2024-001, covering temperature-controlled pharmaceutical transport.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. System handled the typo 'pharamcorp' -> 'PharmaCorp'.

entries.question: ADR certified transport
entries.context: ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement CTR-2024-004: ADR certification required for all drivers. Dangerous goods classes 2, 3, 6.1, 8.
entries.expected_answer: ChemTrans (CTR-2024-004) requires ADR-certified transport for dangerous goods classes 2, 3, 6.1, 8.
entries.generated_answer: ADR certified transport is required under the ChemTrans agreement for hazardous materials.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct identification but omits specific dangerous goods classes.

entries.question: safety
entries.context: Multiple safety documents: ISO 9001 Quality Manual, Driver Safety Protocol, Warehouse Fire Safety Plan, Forklift Operation Manual.
entries.expected_answer: Multiple safety documents are available: ISO 9001 Quality Manual, Driver Safety Protocol, Fire Safety Plan, and Forklift Operation Manual.
entries.generated_answer: LogiCore has several safety-related documents covering quality management, driver safety, fire safety, and forklift operations.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct overview but lacks specific document names and IDs.

entries.question: temperature requirements for pharmaceutical transport
entries.context: PharmaCorp CTR-2024-001: 2-8C cold chain. FreshFoods CTR-2024-002: 0-4C refrigerated.
entries.expected_answer: PharmaCorp requires 2-8C cold chain transport. FreshFoods requires 0-4C refrigerated transport.
entries.generated_answer: For pharmaceutical transport, the temperature must be maintained between 2-8 degrees Celsius as specified in the PharmaCorp agreement.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. PharmaCorp is the pharmaceutical contract. FreshFoods is food, not pharma.

entries.question: which contracts require special driver certifications
entries.context: ChemTrans CTR-2024-004: ADR certification required. Driver Safety Protocol: EU 561/2006 compliance, tachograph training.
entries.expected_answer: ChemTrans (CTR-2024-004) requires ADR certification. Driver Safety Protocol requires EU 561/2006 compliance and tachograph training.
entries.generated_answer: The ChemTrans contract requires ADR certification for dangerous goods transport. Additionally, all drivers must comply with EU 561/2006 driving hours regulations.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Combined information from contract and driver protocol.

entries.question: what is the forklift speed limit in the warehouse
entries.context: Forklift Operation Manual DOC-SAFETY-003: Maximum speed 10 km/h in warehouse areas. 5 km/h in pedestrian zones.
entries.expected_answer: 10 km/h in warehouse areas, 5 km/h in pedestrian zones.
entries.generated_answer: The forklift speed limit is 10 km/h.
entries.scorer_notes: Partially correct. Omits the 5 km/h pedestrian zone limit.

entries.question: where is the emergency assembly point
entries.context: Warehouse Fire Safety Plan DOC-SAFETY-002: Emergency assembly point is parking lot B, northeast corner.
entries.expected_answer: Parking lot B, northeast corner.
entries.generated_answer: The emergency assembly point is in parking lot B.
entries.scorer_notes: Mostly correct but omits 'northeast corner' detail.

entries.question: how much severance do employees get
entries.context: Employee Termination Procedures DOC-HR-004: Severance payment: 0.5 months salary per year of service. Minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months.
entries.expected_answer: 0.5 months salary per year of service, minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months.
entries.generated_answer: Employees receive 0.5 months of salary per year of service as severance, with a minimum of 1 month and maximum of 12 months.
entries.scorer_notes: Complete and accurate.

entries.question: what is the CEO salary
entries.context: Executive Compensation Policy DOC-HR-002: CEO base salary EUR 280,000. Performance bonus up to 40%. Stock options vesting over 4 years.
entries.expected_answer: CEO base salary is EUR 280,000 with up to 40% performance bonus and 4-year vesting stock options.
entries.generated_answer: The CEO salary is EUR 280,000 per year.
entries.scorer_notes: Base salary correct but omits performance bonus (40%) and stock options which are significant compensation components.

entries.question: when should fire extinguishers be inspected
entries.context: Warehouse Fire Safety Plan DOC-SAFETY-002: Fire extinguishers must be inspected every 6 months. Visual checks monthly. Full pressure test annually.
entries.expected_answer: Full inspection every 6 months. Visual checks monthly. Pressure test annually.
entries.generated_answer: Fire extinguishers should be inspected every 6 months.
entries.scorer_notes: Mentions 6-month inspection but misses monthly visual checks and annual pressure test.

entries.question: ISO-9001 Section 4.2
entries.context: ISO 9001 Quality Management Manual DOC-SAFETY-001: Section 4.2 covers document control procedures. All quality documents must be version-controlled.
entries.expected_answer: Section 4.2 of the ISO 9001 manual covers document control procedures with mandatory version control.
entries.generated_answer: ISO 9001 Section 4.2 pertains to document control procedures within the quality management system.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct reference to the specific section.

entries.question: CTR-2024-002
entries.context: FreshFoods Logistics Agreement CTR-2024-002: Refrigerated transport 0-4C. Annual value EUR 960K. Weekly delivery schedule.
entries.expected_answer: FreshFoods Logistics Agreement. Refrigerated transport 0-4C, annual value EUR 960K, weekly deliveries.
entries.generated_answer: CTR-2024-002 is the FreshFoods Logistics Agreement for refrigerated food transport at 0-4 degrees Celsius.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct identification. Missing annual value and delivery schedule.

entries.question: czas pracy kierowcy ciezarowki
entries.context: Driver Safety Protocol DOC-HR-003: Maximum 9 hours driving per day. 45-minute break after 4.5 hours. Weekly maximum 56 hours.
entries.expected_answer: Maksymalny czas jazdy to 9 godzin dziennie, przerwa 45 minut po 4.5 godziny, tygodniowo maksymalnie 56 godzin.
entries.generated_answer: Czas pracy kierowcy to maksymalnie 9 godzin jazdy dziennie z obowiazkowa przerwa po 4.5 godziny.
entries.scorer_notes: Good Polish response. Omits weekly maximum (56 hours).

entries.question: non-perishable transport agreements
entries.context: AutoParts Express Contract CTR-2024-003: Standard freight, no temperature requirements. Auto parts and components.
entries.expected_answer: AutoParts Express (CTR-2024-003) is the non-perishable transport agreement for auto parts and components.
entries.generated_answer: The AutoParts Express contract covers non-perishable transport of auto parts.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Identified the non-perishable contract.

entries.question: new employee first day process
entries.context: Employee Onboarding Handbook DOC-HR-005: First day includes IT setup, safety orientation, team introduction, policy acknowledgment.
entries.expected_answer: First day: IT setup, safety orientation, team introduction, policy acknowledgment.
entries.generated_answer: The new employee process includes receiving IT equipment, a safety orientation tour, meeting the team, and signing policy acknowledgments.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Understood 'first day process' = onboarding. All components mentioned.

entries.question: saftey protocol
entries.context: Driver Safety Protocol DOC-HR-003: Covers pre-trip inspection, driving hours, rest periods, accident reporting.
entries.expected_answer: Driver Safety Protocol includes pre-trip inspection, driving hours compliance, rest periods, and accident reporting.
entries.generated_answer: The safety protocol covers vehicle pre-trip inspections, driving hour limits, mandatory rest periods, and accident reporting procedures.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Handled typo 'saftey' -> 'safety'. Complete answer.

entries.question: SLA breach consequences
entries.context: PharmaCorp Service Agreement CTR-2024-001: SLA breach: 15% penalty per incident. Three consecutive breaches trigger contract review.
entries.expected_answer: SLA breach results in 15% penalty per incident. Three consecutive breaches trigger contract review.
entries.generated_answer: An SLA breach in the PharmaCorp contract incurs a 15% penalty and three consecutive breaches lead to contract review.
entries.scorer_notes: Complete and accurate.

entries.question: contracts
entries.context: Multiple contracts: PharmaCorp CTR-2024-001, FreshFoods CTR-2024-002, AutoParts CTR-2024-003, ChemTrans CTR-2024-004, MSA template.
entries.expected_answer: Five contracts: PharmaCorp, FreshFoods, AutoParts, ChemTrans service agreements plus the Master Service Agreement template.
entries.generated_answer: LogiCore has multiple contracts including service agreements with various clients.
entries.scorer_notes: Too vague. Fails to name any specific contracts or provide useful details.

entries.question: highest penalty per incident across all contracts
entries.context: PharmaCorp: 15% per incident. FreshFoods: 10% per incident. AutoParts: 5% per incident. ChemTrans: EUR 5,000 per incident.
entries.expected_answer: ChemTrans has the highest penalty at EUR 5,000 per incident (absolute). PharmaCorp has the highest percentage at 15%.
entries.generated_answer: The highest penalty is EUR 5,000 per incident in the ChemTrans contract.
entries.scorer_notes: Identifies ChemTrans correctly but doesn't distinguish absolute vs percentage penalties.

entries.question: total annual value of all temperature-controlled contracts
entries.context: PharmaCorp CTR-2024-001: EUR 1.2M annual, 2-8C. FreshFoods CTR-2024-002: EUR 960K annual, 0-4C.
entries.expected_answer: EUR 2.16M total (PharmaCorp EUR 1.2M + FreshFoods EUR 960K).
entries.generated_answer: The total annual value is approximately EUR 2.16 million, combining PharmaCorp (EUR 1.2M) and FreshFoods (EUR 960K).
entries.scorer_notes: Correct calculation combining both contracts.

entries.question: okresy wypowiedzenia umowy o prace
entries.context: Employee Termination Procedures DOC-HR-004: Notice periods per Kodeks Pracy Art. 36: 2 weeks (<6 months), 1 month (6m-3y), 3 months (>3y).
entries.expected_answer: Okresy wypowiedzenia: 2 tygodnie (do 6 miesiecy), 1 miesiac (6m-3 lata), 3 miesiace (powyzej 3 lat).
entries.generated_answer: Okresy wypowiedzenia zgodnie z Kodeksem Pracy: 2 tygodnie dla pracownikow z stazem ponizej 6 miesiecy, 1 miesiac do 3 lat, 3 miesiace powyzej.
entries.scorer_notes: Complete and accurate in Polish. All three notice period tiers mentioned.

entries.question: kontrola jakosci magazyn
entries.context: ISO 9001 Quality Management Manual DOC-SAFETY-001: Warehouse quality control includes incoming inspection, storage conditions monitoring, outgoing verification.
entries.expected_answer: Kontrola jakosci obejmuje: inspekcje przychodzaca, monitoring warunkow przechowywania, weryfikacje wychodzaca.
entries.generated_answer: Kontrola jakosci w magazynie obejmuje kontrole przyjecia towaru oraz monitorowanie warunkow magazynowych.
entries.scorer_notes: Mentions incoming and storage monitoring but omits outgoing verification.

entries.question: executive pay packages

entries.expected_answer: CEO EUR 280K, CFO EUR 220K, COO EUR 200K base salaries with 30-40% performance bonus potential.
entries.generated_answer: Executive compensation at LogiCore includes competitive base salaries with performance bonus potential.
entries.scorer_notes: Too vague. Fails to provide any specific salary figures despite having them in context.

entries.question: contracts that are not for food
entries.context: PharmaCorp: pharma. FreshFoods: food. AutoParts: auto parts. ChemTrans: chemicals.
entries.expected_answer: PharmaCorp (pharma), AutoParts (auto parts), ChemTrans (chemicals). Excludes FreshFoods.
entries.generated_answer: Contracts not involving food include PharmaCorp for pharmaceuticals, AutoParts for auto components, and ChemTrans for chemical transport.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Properly excluded FreshFoods and listed the remaining three.

entries.question: dangerous goods shipping rules
entries.context: ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement CTR-2024-004: ADR certification, UN number labeling, emergency response cards, segregation requirements.
entries.expected_answer: ADR certification required, UN number labeling, emergency response cards, and segregation requirements per ChemTrans agreement.
entries.generated_answer: Dangerous goods must be shipped per ADR regulations with proper certification, labeling, and emergency documentation.
entries.scorer_notes: Good understanding of synonym. Misses segregation requirements.

entries.question: tempature requirements

entries.generated_answer: Temperature requirements are 2-8C for PharmaCorp and 0-4C for FreshFoods.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Handled typo 'tempature'. Both temperature ranges accurate.

entries.question: terminaton procedures

entries.expected_answer: Termination requires documentation, exit interview, IT access revocation, and final payment calculation.
entries.generated_answer: The termination process includes preparing documentation, conducting an exit interview, revoking IT access, and calculating the final payment.
entries.scorer_notes: Complete answer. Typo 'terminaton' correctly resolved.

entries.question: NET 30 payment terms
entries.context: LogiCore Master Service Agreement DOC-LEGAL-005: Standard payment terms NET 30. Late payment interest 1.5% per month.
entries.expected_answer: NET 30 payment terms with 1.5% monthly late payment interest.
entries.generated_answer: LogiCore's standard payment terms are NET 30 days from invoice date.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct on NET 30 but omits late payment interest rate.

entries.question: force majeure clause
entries.context: LogiCore MSA DOC-LEGAL-005: Force majeure includes natural disasters, war, government actions, pandemics. 30-day notification requirement.
entries.expected_answer: Force majeure covers natural disasters, war, government actions, pandemics. 30-day notification required.
entries.generated_answer: The force majeure clause covers events beyond reasonable control including natural disasters and pandemics, with a 30-day notification period.
entries.scorer_notes: Good but omits 'war' and 'government actions' from the list.

entries.question: rules and procedures
entries.context: Multiple docs: driver safety rules, termination procedures, ISO 9001 procedures, fire safety rules, forklift operation rules.
entries.expected_answer: Multiple documents contain rules and procedures: Driver Safety, Termination, ISO 9001, Fire Safety, Forklift Operation.
entries.generated_answer: LogiCore maintains various rules and procedures across safety, HR, and quality management domains.
entries.scorer_notes: Too generic. Should list specific documents when context provides them.

entries.scorer_notes: Correct categories but missing specific document IDs.

entries.generated_answer: LogiCore provides transport logistics services across multiple sectors.

entries.generated_answer: Warehouse operations are governed by quality management, fire safety, and equipment operation guidelines.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct themes but missing specific document names.

entries.question: employees without stock options

entries.expected_answer: Regular employees (covered by DOC-HR-004, DOC-HR-005) do not receive stock options. Only executives (DOC-HR-002) have stock options.
entries.generated_answer: Non-executive employees do not receive stock options based on the HR documentation.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct negation handling.

entries.question: transport without hazmat certification

entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Listed all three non-hazmat contracts.

entries.question: non-confidential employee documents

entries.expected_answer: Only the Employee Onboarding Handbook (clearance 1) is non-confidential.
entries.generated_answer: The Employee Onboarding Handbook is the only non-confidential HR document, available at clearance level 1.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Identified the only clearance-1 document.

entries.question: workplace safety equipment inspection
entries.context: Fire Safety Plan DOC-SAFETY-002: Extinguisher inspection every 6 months. Sprinkler test quarterly. Emergency lighting monthly.
entries.expected_answer: Fire extinguishers: every 6 months. Sprinklers: quarterly. Emergency lighting: monthly.
entries.generated_answer: Safety equipment inspections include fire extinguisher checks every 6 months and sprinkler system tests quarterly.
entries.scorer_notes: Misses emergency lighting monthly checks.

entries.question: contract with the largest annual value
entries.context: PharmaCorp EUR 1.2M, FreshFoods EUR 960K, AutoParts EUR 800K, ChemTrans EUR 2.1M.
entries.expected_answer: ChemTrans at EUR 2.1M annual value is the largest contract.
entries.generated_answer: The ChemTrans Hazmat Agreement has the largest annual value at EUR 2.1 million.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct. Identified ChemTrans as highest value.

entries.question: what happens if a driver exceeds maximum hours and causes a late delivery
entries.context: Driver Safety: max 9h/day, fines for violation. PharmaCorp: 15% late penalty. FreshFoods: 10% late penalty.
entries.expected_answer: Driver faces EU 561/2006 violation fines. Late delivery triggers 15% penalty (PharmaCorp) or 10% (FreshFoods).
entries.generated_answer: If a driver exceeds maximum hours, they violate EU driving regulations and face fines. Any resulting late delivery would trigger contract penalties.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct direction but fails to cite specific penalty percentages despite having them in context.

entries.question: compare penalty structures across all service agreements
entries.context: PharmaCorp: 15%/incident. FreshFoods: 10%/incident. AutoParts: 5%/incident. ChemTrans: EUR 5,000/incident.
entries.expected_answer: PharmaCorp 15%, FreshFoods 10%, AutoParts 5% (percentage-based). ChemTrans EUR 5,000 (fixed amount). Different penalty models.
entries.generated_answer: Penalty structures vary: PharmaCorp charges 15% per incident, FreshFoods 10%, AutoParts 5%, while ChemTrans has a fixed EUR 5,000 penalty. Note the difference between percentage-based and fixed penalties.
entries.scorer_notes: Excellent. Complete comparison with insight about different penalty models.

entries.question: ISO 9001
entries.context: ISO 9001 Quality Management Manual DOC-SAFETY-001: Covers document control, process management, continuous improvement, audit procedures.
entries.expected_answer: ISO 9001 Quality Management Manual covers document control, process management, continuous improvement, and audit procedures.
entries.generated_answer: The ISO 9001 manual covers quality management including process documentation and continuous improvement.
entries.scorer_notes: Misses audit procedures and document control specifics.

entries.question: how long is the probation period for new employees
entries.context: Employee Onboarding Handbook DOC-HR-005: Probation period is 3 months per Kodeks Pracy. Performance review at 1, 2, and 3 months.
entries.expected_answer: 3 months per Kodeks Pracy with performance reviews at months 1, 2, and 3.
entries.generated_answer: The probation period is 3 months in accordance with the Polish Labour Code.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct duration but omits the monthly performance review schedule.

entries.question: EC No 561/2006 driving hours
entries.context: Driver Safety Protocol: EU Regulation 561/2006: max 9h/day, 56h/week. 45-min break after 4.5h continuous driving.
entries.expected_answer: EU 561/2006: 9 hours/day maximum, 56 hours/week, 45-minute break after 4.5 hours continuous driving.
entries.generated_answer: EC Regulation 561/2006 limits driving to 9 hours per day with a 45-minute break required after 4.5 hours of continuous driving.
entries.scorer_notes: Correct on daily and break limits but omits weekly 56-hour maximum.

entries.question: what are the penalty fees for late deliveries
entries.context: PharmaCorp: 15% per incident. FreshFoods: 10% per incident. AutoParts: 5% per incident.
entries.expected_answer: PharmaCorp 15%, FreshFoods 10%, AutoParts 5% per incident.
entries.generated_answer: Late delivery penalties vary by contract: PharmaCorp charges 15%, FreshFoods 10%, and AutoParts 5% per incident.
